title: Safe/Degraded Mode — Phase 1 & 2 Implementation Plan
consumer_purpose: Enable the OpenCode desktop app to open with a functional diagnostic UI when critical subsystems (sidecar, PGlite, or migrations) fail during startup, and provide a health registry for feature-level degradation awareness.
content:
{
  "plan_id": "safe-degraded-mode-phases-1-2",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "author": "architect",
  "status": "design",
  "created_at": "2026-05-30",
  "revision": 1,
  "based_on": "safe-degraded-mode-init.v1.json",
  "focus": "Phases 1 and 2 — Init flow restructuring and Service Health Registry",

  "executive_summary": "This plan designs the implementation for Phases 1 (Init Flow Restructuring) and 2 (Service Health Registry) of the Safe/Degraded Mode architecture. The critical architectural insight is that Safe Mode must work WITHOUT the sidecar server — the Electron main process creates a loading window that transitions to safe mode UI when sidecar spawning fails. No secondary process needed. The Health Registry is an Effect Service that extends the existing /global/health endpoint with per-component breakdown, enabling feature-level degradation in Phase 4.",

  "design_decisions": [
    {
      "id": "DD-001",
      "decision": "Safe Mode reuses the loading window's BrowserWindow — navigates from loading.html to safe-mode.html when sidecar fails",
      "rationale": "No new BrowserWindow creation needed. The loading window already has the preload bridge wired and sandbox configured. Loading safe-mode.html via loadWindow(win, 'safe-mode.html') navigates the same window. The renderer protocol handler serves files from packages/desktop/src/renderer/. This is more surgical than creating a new window because it requires zero new BrowserWindow configuration, no new sandbox setup, and no new IPC wiring.",
      "pattern_used": "Existing loadWindow(win, html) pattern at windows.ts:260-269"
    },
    {
      "id": "DD-002",
      "decision": "Error classification in sidecar uses error message string matching, not a shared error type enum",
      "rationale": "The sidecar and main process are separate processes communicating via IPC messages (serialized JSON). Adding a shared enum would require either a shared type package or duplicated constant definitions. String matching on error messages (detecting 'PGlite', 'DuckDB', 'Migration', etc.) is simpler and avoids any cross-package dependency.",
      "pattern_used": "Existing serializeError() pattern at sidecar.ts"
    },
    {
      "id": "DD-003",
      "decision": "Safe mode detection runs in two phases: (1) synchronous check for --safe-mode flag and stale lock file BEFORE spawn, (2) asynchronous handling of sidecar spawn failure",
      "rationale": "The --safe-mode CLI flag and previous-crash lock file can be checked synchronously at the top of the main Effect generator, before any sidecar process is spawned. This avoids spawning a process we know will fail. Sidecar spawn failure is handled asynchronously via Effect.catch around the spawn+health-check block.",
      "pattern_used": "Existing Effect.gen structure at index.ts:107-391"
    },
    {
      "id": "DD-004",
      "decision": "HealthRegistry is an Effect Service with a Ref-based ConcurrentMap of component → status, exposed via the existing /global/health endpoint",
      "rationale": "The existing health endpoint already returns {healthy: true, version}. Extending it with a components breakdown is a schema extension, not a new endpoint. The HealthRegistry service holds the source of truth. Components register at init time. The renderer polls this extended endpoint via the existing useServerHealth hook. No new IPC channel needed.",
      "pattern_used": "Existing Context.Service pattern used by all opencode services"
    },
    {
      "id": "DD-005",
      "decision": "Plugin quarantine uses config-persisted crash counts, auto-disables after 3 consecutive crashes",
      "rationale": "Plugin crash counts must survive app restart. In-memory counters reset on restart, allowing a flaky plugin to repeatedly crash. Persisting crash counts in the Config system means quarantine carries across sessions. User can re-enable from plugin settings, which resets the counter. Threshold of 3 consecutive crashes prevents false positives from one-off failures.",
      "pattern_used": "Existing Config system at packages/opencode/src/config/"
    }
  ],

  "phases": {
    "phase_1": {
      "name": "Init Flow Restructuring",
      "surgical_rationale": "Only 5 files need substantive changes beyond the new safe-mode renderer. The smallest fix is wrapping PGlite (3 lines). The critical behavioral change is the Effect.catch around sidecar spawn (8 lines).",

      "fixes": [
        {
          "id": "F01-PGlite-protection",
          "description": "Wrap new PGlite(dataDir) in try/catch with component-identified error",
          "severity": "critical",
          "target": "dp-02",
          "files": ["packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts:24"],
          "before": "const client = new PGlite(dataDir)\nreturn drizzle({ client }) as PgliteDatabase",
          "after": "let client: PGlite\ntry {\n  client = new PGlite(dataDir)\n} catch (cause) {\n  throw new DatabaseInitError(\"PGlite initialization failed\", {\n    cause: cause instanceof Error ? cause : new Error(String(cause)),\n    dataDir,\n  })\n}\nreturn drizzle({ client }) as PgliteDatabase",
          "risks": [
            "DatabaseInitError class must be defined (small NamedError subclass, ~10 lines). Add in same file or a shared errors module.",
            "If PGlite constructor throws asynchronously (rejected promise), the try/catch won't catch it. Check PGlite type signature: new PGlite() is synchronous per the PGlite API, so this should be safe."
          ],
          "validation": "Run existing db.pg.ts tests with corrupt data directory — should throw DatabaseInitError, not crash."
        },
        {
          "id": "F02-InitStep-extension",
          "description": "Extend InitStep discriminated union with safe_mode and degraded_mode phases, add SystemHealth and SafeModeActions types",
          "severity": "medium",
          "target": "dp-01",
          "files": ["packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts:3"],
          "before": "export type InitStep = { phase: \"server_waiting\" } | { phase: \"sqlite_waiting\" } | { phase: \"done\" }",
          "after": "export type InitStep =\n  | { phase: \"server_waiting\" }\n  | { phase: \"sqlite_waiting\" }\n  | { phase: \"safe_mode\"; error: { message: string; component: string } }\n  | { phase: \"degraded_mode\" }\n  | { phase: \"done\" }",
          "risks": [
            "loading.tsx currently matches phase === 'done' — must handle safe_mode phase or will show no UI.",
            "Any existing code that exhaustively switches on InitStep must be updated to handle new variants."
          ],
          "validation": "TypeScript checks pass for all InitStep consumers. loading.tsx handles safe_mode phase."
        },
        {
          "id": "F03-Sidecar-error-classification",
          "description": "Add component field to sidecar error messages, add classifyErrorComponent helper",
          "severity": "high",
          "target": "dp-01, dp-03, dp-08",
          "files": ["packages/desktop/src/main/sidecar.ts:26", "packages/desktop/src/main/sidecar.ts:87"],
          "before_type": "type SidecarMessage =\n  | { type: \"sqlite\"; progress: SqliteMigrationProgress }\n  | { type: \"ready\" }\n  | { type: \"stopped\" }\n  | { type: \"error\"; error: { message: string; stack?: string } }",
          "after_type": "type SidecarMessage =\n  | { type: \"sqlite\"; progress: SqliteMigrationProgress }\n  | { type: \"ready\" }\n  | { type: \"stopped\" }\n  | { type: \"error\"; error: { message: string; stack?: string }; component: string }",
          "before_catch": "catch (error) {\n  parentPort.postMessage({ type: \"error\", error: serializeError(error) })\n  setImmediate(() => process.exit(1))\n}",
          "after_catch": "catch (error) {\n  const err = error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error))\n  const component = /PGlite|Database/i.test(err.message) ? \"db\"\n    : /migrat/i.test(err.message) ? \"migration\"\n    : /DuckDB/i.test(err.message) ? \"duckdb\"\n    : /listen|Server/i.test(err.message) ? \"server\"\n    : \"unknown\"\n  parentPort.postMessage({ type: \"error\", error: serializeError(err), component })\n  setImmediate(() => process.exit(1))\n}",
          "risks": [
            "Error message matching is fragile — a reworded PGlite error upstream would break classification. Mitigation: wrap in DatabaseInitError (Fix 01) with stable prefix.",
            "If error is a plain object (not Error), serializeError handles it but string matching may miss."
          ],
          "validation": "Simulate sidecar init failures with different error sources — verify component string in emitted SidecarMessage."
        },
        {
          "id": "F04-Spawn-error-propagation",
          "description": "Extend spawnLocalServer to propagate component info through the error chain",
          "severity": "high",
          "target": "dp-01",
          "files": ["packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:14", "packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:120"],
          "before_parse": "} else if (parsed.type === \"error\") {\n  cleanup()\n  reject(new Error(parsed.error.message))\n}",
          "after_parse": "} else if (parsed.type === \"error\") {\n  cleanup()\n  const err = new Error(parsed.error.message)\n  ;(err as any).component = parsed.component ?? \"unknown\"\n  reject(err)\n}",
          "risks": [
            "Attaching .component to Error is a soft pattern — TypeScript won't catch misspellings. Consider a typed SafeModeError class.",
            "If Effect wraps the error, .component may be lost. Effect.Error preserves cause chains, so wrapping errors should propagate. Use Effect.catchAllDefect to inspect."
          ],
          "validation": "Unit test: verify spawnLocalServer rejects with error.component === 'db' when sidecar sends {type:'error', component:'db'}."
        },
        {
          "id": "F05-Window-transition-helper",
          "description": "Add transitionToSafeMode() helper in windows.ts",
          "severity": "medium",
          "target": "dp-01, dp-08",
          "files": ["packages/desktop/src/main/windows.ts:185"],
          "before": "// No safe mode window function exists",
          "after": "export function transitionToSafeMode(win: BrowserWindow, error: { message: string; component: string }): BrowserWindow {\n  win.setSize(800, 600)\n  win.center()\n  win.setResizable(true)\n  win.setTitle('OpenCode — Safe Mode')\n  loadWindow(win, 'safe-mode.html')\n  return win\n}",
          "risks": [
            "loadWindow is private to windows.ts — transitionToSafeMode must be in same file.",
            "Setting window size before navigation may cause visual flash. Mitigation: set size, then load content."
          ],
          "validation": "Call transitionToSafeMode with a mock BrowserWindow — verify it calls loadWindow with 'safe-mode.html'."
        },
        {
          "id": "F06-Safe-mode-renderer",
          "description": "Create safe-mode.tsx and safe-mode.html for the diagnostic display panel",
          "severity": "high",
          "target": "dp-01",
          "new_files": [
            "packages/desktop/src/renderer/safe-mode.tsx",
            "packages/desktop/src/renderer/safe-mode.html",
            "packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/safe-mode-card.tsx"
          ],
          "design": "safe-mode.tsx is a SolidJS component that: 1) calls window.api.getSafeModeDiagnostics() on mount; 2) shows diagnostic summary panel (error message, system info); 3) renders action cards for each safe mode action; 4) each card calls window.api.safeModeAction(actionName); 5) includes 'Retry Normal Startup' button that relaunches. safe-mode.html is an HTML shell loading safe-mode.tsx, identical to loading.html pattern.",
          "risks": [
            "Depends on IPC methods from Fix 07 — must be created in same batch.",
            "Must verify build system picks up new .tsx and .html files from renderer/ directory."
          ],
          "validation": "Open safe-mode.html directly via oc://renderer/safe-mode.html — verify it renders diagnostic panel and cards invoke IPC."
        },
        {
          "id": "F07-Safe-mode-IPC",
          "description": "Add IPC channels, preload bridge methods, and main process handlers for safe mode actions",
          "severity": "high",
          "target": "dp-01",
          "files": [
            "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-channels.ts:74",
            "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-contract.ts:130",
            "packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts:65",
            "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-init.ts:42"
          ],
          "design": {
            "new_channels": [
              "IPC.handle.GET_SAFE_MODE_DIAGNOSTICS",
              "IPC.handle.SAFE_MODE_ACTION"
            ],
            "preload_methods": {
              "getSafeModeDiagnostics": "typedInvoke(IPC.handle.GET_SAFE_MODE_DIAGNOSTICS)",
              "safeModeAction": "(action: SafeModeAction) => typedInvoke(IPC.handle.SAFE_MODE_ACTION, action)"
            },
            "SafeModeAction type": "export type SafeModeAction = 'export_debug_logs' | 'open_logs' | 'repair_database' | 'disable_plugins' | 'disable_mcp' | 'clear_stale_locks' | 'reset_config' | 'copy_diagnostic_summary' | 'retry_normal_startup'",
            "SafeModeDiagnostics type": "export type SafeModeDiagnostics = { error: { message: string; component: string }; systemInfo: { platform: string; arch: string; version: string; userDataPath: string; logPath: string } }"
          },
          "risks": [
            "IpcHandleContract requires strict typing — both new channels need {params, returns} definitions or TypeScript errors.",
            "'retry_normal_startup' action must delete crash lock file before relaunch to prevent infinite safe mode loop."
          ],
          "validation": "Invoke each IPC endpoint from preload bridge — verify main process handlers execute correctly."
        },
        {
          "id": "F08-Main-process-safe-mode-routing",
          "description": "Add --safe-mode flag, crash detection from stale lock file, and Effect.catch around sidecar spawn routing to safe mode",
          "severity": "critical",
          "target": "dp-01, dp-08",
          "files": [
            "packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts:55",
            "packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts:107",
            "packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts:309",
            "packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts:364"
          ],
          "design": {
            "check_1": "Check OPENCODE_SAFE_MODE env var or process.argv for --safe-mode at top of main generator. Set const safeModeRequested.",
            "check_2": "Check for stale lock file: fs.existsSync(path.join(userDataPath, '.crash_lock')). If found, set crashDetected = true. Lock file created by sidecar at start(), deleted on clean shutdown.",
            "insertion_point_1": "After app.whenReady() but before sidecar spawn: if (safeModeRequested || crashDetected) { skip spawn, go to safe mode }",
            "insertion_point_2": "Wrap spawn+health-check in Effect.catch instead of letting error propagate: yield* Effect.catch(spawnBlock, (error) => { transitionToSafeMode(overlay, error); return Effect.void })",
            "lock_file": "In sidecar.ts start(), after Server.listen(): writeFileSync(lockPath, String(pid)). In stop(): unlinkSync(lockPath). In main before-quit handler: unlinkSync if sidecar stopped cleanly."
          },
          "before": "// No safe mode handling — spawn failure crashes the entire Effect generator",
          "after": "{\n  const safeModeRequested = process.argv.includes('--safe-mode') || process.env.OPENCODE_SAFE_MODE === '1'\n  const crashLock = path.join(userDataPath, '.crash_lock')\n  const crashDetected = fs.existsSync(crashLock)\n  if (safeModeRequested || crashDetected) {\n    yield* Effect.sync(() => {\n      overlay = transitionToSafeMode(createLoadingWindow(), {\n        message: safeModeRequested ? 'Safe mode requested' : 'Previous session crashed',\n        component: 'system'\n      })\n    })\n    return\n  }\n  // ... existing spawn code wrapped in Effect.catch ...\n  yield* Effect.catch(loadingTask, (error) =>\n    Effect.sync(() => {\n      overlay = transitionToSafeMode(overlay ?? createLoadingWindow(), {\n        message: (error as any).message ?? String(error),\n        component: (error as any).component ?? 'sidecar'\n      })\n    })\n  )\n}",
          "risks": [
            "Effect.catch catches ALL errors from spawn block — narrow scope to only wrap spawn+health-check, not unrelated setup.",
            "Loading window may not exist when safe mode triggers — code creates it on demand as fallback.",
            "Effect.catch return types: guarded block returns void (fiber), catch must also return void."
          ],
          "validation": [
            "Test with OPENCODE_SAFE_MODE=1 — app shows safe mode instantly, no sidecar.",
            "Test with stale .crash_lock — app detects crash, enters safe mode.",
            "Test with corrupt DB — sidecar fails, main catches, transitions to safe mode."
          ]
        }
      ]
    },

    "phase_2": {
      "name": "Service Health Registry",
      "surgical_rationale": "One new file (~100 lines), all other changes are surgical insertions: extending health schema (3 lines), handler (4 lines), wiring (2 lines), and 1-3 line health reporting calls.",

      "fixes": [
        {
          "id": "F09-HealthRegistry-service",
          "description": "Create HealthRegistry Effect service tracking per-component health status",
          "severity": "critical",
          "target": "dp-04, dp-05, dp-06",
          "new_files": ["packages/opencode/src/server/health.ts"],
          "estimated_lines": 110,
          "design": {
            "excerpt": "export enum HealthStatus { Healthy = \"healthy\", Degraded = \"degraded\", Down = \"down\", Unknown = \"unknown\" }\nexport interface ComponentHealth { status: HealthStatus; message?: string; updatedAt: number }\nexport interface Interface {\n  readonly get: (component: string) => Effect.Effect<ComponentHealth>\n  readonly getAll: () => Effect.Effect<Record<string, ComponentHealth>>\n  readonly set: (component: string, status: ComponentHealth) => Effect.Effect<void>\n}\nexport class Service extends Context.Service<Service, Interface>()(\"@opencode/HealthRegistry\") {}\nexport const layer: Layer.Layer<Service> = Layer.effect(Service, Effect.gen(function* () {\n  const state = yield* Ref.make<Map<string, ComponentHealth>>(new Map())\n  return {\n    get: (c) => Ref.get(state).pipe(Effect.map((m) => m.get(c) ?? { status: HealthStatus.Unknown, updatedAt: Date.now() })),\n    getAll: () => Ref.get(state).pipe(Effect.map((m) => Object.fromEntries(m))),\n    set: (c, h) => Ref.update(state, (m) => { m.set(c, h); return m }),\n  }\n}))",
            "component_constants": "DB = 'db', MCP = 'mcp', PLUGIN = 'plugin', DUCKDB = 'duckdb', SERVER = 'server', AGENT = 'agent'"
          },
          "risks": [
            "Ref.update mutates the Map in place — must use new Map(m).set() for immutable update or ensure Ref semantics are understood.",
            "Component name strings must be consistent. Export constants from health.ts."
          ],
          "validation": "Unit test: create HealthRegistry via layer, register 3 components, query statuses, verify correct values."
        },
        {
          "id": "F10-Health-endpoint-extension",
          "description": "Extend /global/health schema and handler with per-component health",
          "severity": "high",
          "target": "dp-04, dp-05",
          "files": [
            "packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/global.ts:9",
            "packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/global.ts:75"
          ],
          "before_schema": "const GlobalHealth = Schema.Struct({ healthy: Schema.Literal(true), version: Schema.String })",
          "after_schema": "const ComponentHealth = Schema.Struct({\n  status: Schema.Literal('healthy', 'degraded', 'down', 'unknown'),\n  message: Schema.optional(Schema.String),\n  updatedAt: Schema.Number,\n})\nconst GlobalHealth = Schema.Struct({\n  healthy: Schema.Literal(true),\n  version: Schema.String,\n  components: Schema.optional(Schema.Record({ key: Schema.String, value: ComponentHealth })),\n})",
          "before_handler": "const health = Effect.fn(\"GlobalHttpApi.health\")(function* () {\n  return { healthy: true as const, version: InstallationVersion }\n})",
          "after_handler": "const health = Effect.fn(\"GlobalHttpApi.health\")(function* () {\n  const registry = yield* HealthRegistry.Service\n  const components = yield* registry.getAll()\n  return { healthy: true as const, version: InstallationVersion, components }\n})",
          "risks": [
            "Handler now depends on HealthRegistry.Service — server layer must provide it or handler fails at runtime.",
            "Optional Schema.Record serializes as undefined when empty — verify backward compatibility with existing clients."
          ],
          "validation": "GET /global/health returns components map when HealthRegistry registered. Same endpoint still works without HealthRegistry (missing service caught gracefully)."
        },
        {
          "id": "F11-Wire-HealthRegistry-into-server",
          "description": "Add HealthRegistry.layer to server layer composition",
          "severity": "high",
          "target": "dp-04, dp-05",
          "files": ["packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts:192"],
          "before": "Layer.provide([\n  Account.defaultLayer, Agent.defaultLayer, ...\n])",
          "after": "Layer.provide([\n  HealthRegistry.layer,\n  Account.defaultLayer, Agent.defaultLayer, ...\n])",
          "risks": [
            "Layer ordering: HealthRegistry must be first so components can register during their own init.",
            "MCP and Plugin layers don't currently depend on HealthRegistry. Use Effect.serviceOption pattern for soft dependency."
          ],
          "validation": "Server starts with HealthRegistry provided. Components skip reporting if registry unavailable."
        },
        {
          "id": "F12-Component-health-reporting",
          "description": "Add health reporting calls to PGlite, DuckDB, MCP, and Plugin init",
          "severity": "medium",
          "target": "dp-04, dp-05, dp-06",
          "files": [
            "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts:29",
            "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts:240",
            "packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts:557",
            "packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:165"
          ],
          "pattern": "const registry = yield* Effect.serviceOption(HealthRegistry.Service)\nif (Option.isSome(registry)) {\n  yield* registry.value.set('db', { status: HealthStatus.Healthy, updatedAt: Date.now() })\n}",
          "risks": [
            "DuckDB currently has no failure path (WASM stubbed to null, always falls back to CLI). Degraded path won't be exercised until WASM is implemented.",
            "MCP per-server reporting requires iterating config+status map. Aggregate to overall MCP health."
          ],
          "validation": "Start server with all healthy — GET /global/health shows all components healthy. Simulate component failure — health reflects down status."
        },
        {
          "id": "F13-Plugin-crash-counters-and-quarantine",
          "description": "Add persistent crash counters per plugin, auto-quarantine after 3 consecutive crashes",
          "severity": "medium",
          "target": "dp-06",
          "files": ["packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:135", "packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:288"],
          "before_trigger": "for (const hook of s.hooks) {\n  const fn = hook[name] as any\n  if (!fn) continue\n  yield* Effect.promise(async () => fn(input, output))\n}",
          "after_trigger": "for (const hook of s.hooks) {\n  const fn = hook[name] as any\n  if (!fn) continue\n  const result = yield* Effect.promise(async () => fn(input, output)).pipe(Effect.exit)\n  if (Exit.isFailure(result)) {\n    yield* incrementCrashCount(hook.id)\n    if (yield* getCrashCount(hook.id) >= 3) {\n      yield* quarantinePlugin(hook.id)\n    }\n  } else {\n    yield* resetCrashCount(hook.id)\n  }\n}",
          "risks": [
            "BEHAVIOR CHANGE: Currently one crashing hook STOPS the loop. New behavior CONTINUES on error so other hooks still fire. This is a deliberate improvement but must be documented.",
            "Crash counts persist in Config JSONC. Must reload from config on plugin init to survive restart.",
            "Threshold of 3 'consecutive' = without successful invocation in between. Reset on success logic handles this."
          ],
          "validation": "Unit test: create crashing plugin, call trigger 3 times → crashCount=3, plugin quarantined. Non-crashing call resets counter."
        }
      ]
    }
  },

  "apply_order": [
    "F01-PGlite-protection",
    "F02-InitStep-extension",
    "F03-Sidecar-error-classification",
    "F04-Spawn-error-propagation",
    "F05-Window-transition-helper",
    "F06-Safe-mode-renderer",
    "F07-Safe-mode-IPC",
    "F08-Main-process-safe-mode-routing",
    "F09-HealthRegistry-service",
    "F10-Health-endpoint-extension",
    "F11-Wire-HealthRegistry-into-server",
    "F12-Component-health-reporting",
    "F13-Plugin-crash-counters-and-quarantine"
  ],

  "file_inventory": {
    "modified_files": [
      "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/sidecar.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/windows.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-channels.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-contract.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts",
      "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-init.ts",
      "packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/global.ts",
      "packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/global.ts",
      "packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts",
      "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts",
      "packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts",
      "packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts"
    ],
    "new_files": [
      "packages/opencode/src/server/health.ts (~110 lines)",
      "packages/desktop/src/renderer/safe-mode.tsx (~180 lines)",
      "packages/desktop/src/renderer/safe-mode.html (~22 lines)",
      "packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/safe-mode-card.tsx (~40 lines)"
    ],
    "estimated_total_lines": "~700 new lines across phases 1-2"
  },

  "risks": [
    {
      "risk": "Effect.catch swallows errors that should propagate",
      "severity": "high",
      "mitigation": "Only wrap the spawn+health-check block, not the entire main generator."
    },
    {
      "risk": "Plugin trigger behavior changes from 'stop on first error' to 'continue on error'",
      "severity": "medium",
      "mitigation": "This is a deliberate improvement (DP-06). Document in change notes."
    },
    {
      "risk": "safe-mode.html not picked up by build system",
      "severity": "high",
      "mitigation": "Verify build config in packages/desktop. If glob-based, new files are auto-included. If manifest-based, add entries explicitly."
    },
    {
      "risk": "HealthRegistry optional dependency pattern is fragile for init-time registration",
      "severity": "low",
      "mitigation": "Effect.serviceOption is canonical for optional services. Returns None if not provided — safe."
    },
    {
      "risk": "Lock file writes in sidecar may conflict with filesystem permissions",
      "severity": "low",
      "mitigation": "userDataPath is guaranteed writable by the Electron app. Wrap in try/catch with Effect.ignore on failure."
    }
  ],

  "validation_plan": {
    "smoke_tests": [
      {
        "step": 1,
